news.title: DDA launches second phase of Jan Sadharan Awaas Yojana housing scheme
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority has rolled out the second phase of its Jan Sadharan Awaas Yojana housing initiative, offering around 1,500 budget-friendly flats across well-connected regions of Delhi. Designed with low-income and economically disadvantaged families in mind, the scheme has sparked significant enthusiasm, with many units snapped up on the very first day.

news.title: IndiQube Spaces' posts loss of ₹30 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: IndiQube Spaces Ltd announced a net loss of Rs 29.86 crore for the second quarter of this fiscal year. This figure is notably better than the Rs 52.52 crore loss recorded in the equivalent quarter last year. The company’s income surged impressively, reaching Rs 366.59 crore.

news.title: Anant Raj's profit up 31% to ₹138 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a remarkable financial update, Anant Raj Ltd has seen its consolidated net profit soar by 31% to reach Rs 138.18 crore for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. Furthermore, the company's total income climbed to Rs 640.89 crore.

news.title: Pioneer Urban Land buys 9,419 sq ft flat in DLF's Camellias for ₹95 crore
news.para: Pioneer Urban Land and Infrastructure Ltd made headlines with its latest acquisition: a lavish 9,419 sq ft residence within DLF’s exclusive The Camellias project in Gurugram, valued at an impressive Rs 95 crore. Registered on September 29, this transaction underscores the furious demand for luxury homes among the ultra-wealthy.

news.title: Pune: PMC issues guidelines for old properties’ safe demolition ahead of redevelopment
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation mandates developers to inform fire brigade and neighbors before demolishing old structures. Neighboring properties must be vacated if at risk of damage. Structural engineer approval is now compulsory, and debris transport requires green cloth covers. These measures aim to prevent past mishaps and pollution during redevelopment.

news.title: About 25 acres of Aravali forest cleared of farmhouses, walls
news.para: Forest officials in Gurugram cleared approximately 25 acres of encroached land in the Raisina Hills of Sohna on Friday. This demolition drive targeted illegal farmhouses and structures built on protected Aravali land, aiming to restore ecological integrity. Further actions are planned following public notices.

news.title: YEIDA gives one year relief to plot allottees, launches new settlement policy
news.para: YEIDA has granted a one-year extension until December 31, 2026, for residential plot construction, benefiting allottees facing delays due to pending infrastructure work. This decision supports thousands of plot holders across five sectors ahead of the Noida International Airport's launch. The authority also approved a new one-time settlement policy and the design for its new headquarters.

news.title: Maps to NOCs, one window for all your approvals soon in Ghaziabad
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority is set to replace its Online Building Plan Approval System (OBPAS) with the new Fast and Simplified Trust Based Plan Approval System (FASTPAS). This streamlined process will offer a single window for approvals, including NOCs from various departments, and is expected to double the map approval rate.

news.title: Probe why Wadala slum rehabilitation delayed for 20 years: Bombay HC
news.para: Bombay High Court has ordered the SRA CEO to investigate a 20-year delay in a Wadala West slum rehabilitation scheme. The court also directed the BMC chief to coordinate with the SRA CEO on the matter. This action follows a petition regarding transit rent payments.

news.title: Zomato leases 2.7 lakh sq ft office space in Gurugram
news.para: Zomato has leased 270,000 sq ft at Tata Realty's Intellion Park in Gurugram and is in talks for an additional 1 million sq ft, signaling significant office space expansion. This move highlights robust demand for commercial real estate in India's top cities, driven by GCCs, flexible workspaces, and tech firm growth.

news.title: Qatar National Bank renews office lease at ₹775 per sq ft per month at Mumbai's BKC
news.para: Qatar National Bank (QNB) has renewed its lease for office space at Mumbai's Bandra Kurla Complex (BKC), at a monthly rent of Rs 775 per sq ft, making it one of the highest commercial lease rentals recorded anywhere in the country. The 5-year agreement includes an escalation clause to increase rentals by 4.5% every year.

news.title: UK house prices rose 0.6% in October 2025: Halifax
news.para: In October, UK house prices experienced their most significant monthly increase since January, reaching unprecedented heights. Buyer interest remains strong, even as analysts note the housing market's resilience against possible tax hikes. With mortgage rates trending downwards, prices could continue to climb in the upcoming months.

news.title: SC flags deficiencies, asks law panel to examine blockchain technology for property registration
news.para: Addressing deep-rooted challenges in colonial property legislation, the Supreme Court is advocating for sweeping reforms at the national level. The court's directive to the Law Commission includes the exploration of Blockchain technology for property registration, aiming to foster a more secure and transparent framework.

news.title: Blackstone entity gets CCI approval to acquire 80% stake in Aadhar Housing Finance
news.para: In a significant stride for the housing finance sector, the Competition Commission of India has given its nod to Blackstone’s BCP Asia II Holdco VII Pte Ltd. This entity is set to acquire a commanding 80.15% stake in Aadhar Housing Finance Ltd.

news.title: SC orders inquiry into stalled Greater Noida housing project, appoints ex-HC judge to head probe
news.para: In a significant move, the Supreme Court has initiated a special committee tasked with addressing the long-dormant housing project in Greater Noida. With hundreds of eager homebuyers having awaited their dream homes for nearly 20 years, the committee's goal is to pinpoint legitimate property holders and devise a strategy to move the project forward.

news.title: JSW Cement posts net profit of ₹75 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: JSW Cement reported a net consolidated profit of ₹75.36 crore for Q2 FY26, with total income at ₹1,460.06 crore. The company saw a 15% year-on-year volume growth to 3.11 million tonnes. Significant debt reduction was achieved due to IPO proceeds, and a new grinding unit in Sambalpur was commissioned.

news.title: Puravankara's net loss widens to ₹43 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Puravankara reported a net consolidated loss of ₹42.99 crore for Q2 FY26, despite a 27.53% increase in total income to ₹662.73 crore. The company achieved pre-sales of ₹1,322 crore, driven by strong sustenance sales, and strengthened its development pipeline by adding over 6.36 million sq. ft.

news.title: Somany Ceramics' net profit dips 29% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Somany Ceramics reported a 29.01% decline in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, with profit after tax at ₹12.31 crore.  Despite this, total income saw a 3.18% growth to ₹688.85 crore. The company also approved a draft scheme of amalgamation and completed the acquisition of a 51% stake in Dura Build Care.

news.title: Signature Global posts loss of ₹46.86 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Signatureglobal (India) reported a net consolidated loss of ₹46.86 crore for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, a significant shift from a profit of ₹4.15 crore in the previous fiscal's same quarter. The company's total income also saw a substantial decrease of 52.08% to ₹372.51 crore.

news.title: Aadhar Housing Finance net profit grows 17.12% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Aadhar Housing Finance reported a 17.12% rise in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, reaching ₹266.47 crore. The company's total income grew by 17.66% to ₹899.26 crore. Assets under management increased by 21% to ₹27,554 crore, driven by strong operational performance and demand in the affordable housing segment.
